name: SIGMA - Repeated Failed Sign-Ins (Windows Security 4625) [threshold]
description: Elastic threshold companion for rules/sigma/auth_win_bruteforce_failed_logons.yml (issue #192). Alerts when a single target account (winlog.event_data.TargetUserName) accrues >=5 Security 4625 failed-logon events within a 5-minute window. The Sigma file is the documented logic-of-record (single-event definition of a failed logon); this threshold rule adds the count that sigma_eval.py / the lucene conversion target cannot express. Lookback window ("from": "now-6m") deliberately exceeds the run interval ("interval": "5m") so consecutive evaluations overlap by 1 minute — a non-overlapping (tumbling) 5m/5m window would let an attacker straddle two scheduled runs and stay under threshold in either one while exceeding it in any real sliding 5-minute view.
author: Suburban-SOC
false_positives: High-volume legitimate failures during a mass password-reset rollout.
Load-balanced authentication proxies retrying with stale credentials.
